nodes.context: English crime gang based in South London in the 1960s, involved in racketeering, extortion, torture, and other crimes.
London-based criminal gang led by the Kray twins, rivals of the Richardson Gang.
East End gang that George Cornell originally belonged to.
Manchester-based criminal group; Dougie Flood was an alleged member.

edges.evidence_quote: The Richardson gang and the Kray twins were engaged in a turf war in the mid-to-late 1960s.
edges.context: The two gangs were in a turf war, a conflict relationship.
The infobox lists the Kray Firm as rivals of the Richardson Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Cornell was originally a member of an East End gang called "The Watney Streeters" and later became involved with the Krays. However, he changed sides around 1964 and allied himself with the Richardsons.
edges.context: George Cornell moved from the Watney Streeters to ally with the Richardson Gang.
